sourceMovie.title: Gangster Squad
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Gangster Squad for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ruben Fleischer keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tango & Cash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gangster Squad clicked for you, Tango & Cash (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mesrine: Killer Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gangster Squad clicked for you, Mesrine: Killer Instinct (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gangster Squad?
faq.answer: Try Tango & Cash, Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Colors—each shares DNA with Gangster Squad while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gangster Squad?
faq.answer: Gangster Squad (2013) is best described as Crime, Drama, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gangster Squad worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
